front: Tenho certeza absoluta de que o servidor está online.
pronunciation: TEYNG-yoo sehr-TEH-zah awb-soo-LOO-tah jee kee oo sehr-vee-DOHR ees-TAH ohn-LY-nee.
back: I have absolute certainty that the server is online.
context: Using 'ter certeza absoluta de que' to express definitive, unshakeable certainty.

front: Você tem certeza disso?
pronunciation: voh-SEH cheyng sehr-TEH-zah DEES-oo?
back: Are you sure about that?
context: A standard conversational question to verify someone's certainty level. Contraction: de + isso = disso.

front: Eu não tenho certeza se vou conseguir terminar hoje.
pronunciation: ew nohng TEYNG-yoo sehr-TEH-zah see vow kohn-seh-GEER tehr-mee-NAHR OH-zhee.
back: I'm not sure if I'll be able to finish today.
context: Expressing schedule uncertainty using 'não tenho certeza se' followed by the future proximity construction.

front: Eu duvido que eles mudem o prazo final.
pronunciation: ew doo-VEE-doo kee EH-leys MOO-dehng oo PRAH-zoo fee-NAW.
back: I doubt that they will change the final deadline.
context: The verb 'duvidar que' (to doubt that) inherently triggers the subjunctive form ('mudem') in the following clause.

front: Talvez seja melhor revisar o código-fonte.
pronunciation: taw-VEHS SEH-zhah meh-LYOHR heh-vee-ZAHR oo KOH-jee-goo FOHN-chee.
back: Maybe it's better to review the source code.
context: The adverb 'talvez' (maybe/perhaps) requires the subjunctive mood ('seja') to indicate doubt or potentiality.

front: É provável que a reunião seja cancelada.
pronunciation: eh proo-VAH-vew kee ah hehoo-nee-OHNG SEH-zhah kahn-seh-LAH-dah.
back: It is likely that the meeting will be canceled.
context: Using the impersonal expression 'é provável que' to evaluate situational probability. Triggers the subjunctive 'seja'.

front: Não há dúvida de que ela é a melhor candidata.
pronunciation: nohng ah DOO-vee-dah jee kee EH-lah eh ah meh-LYOHR kahn-jee-DAH-tah.
back: There is no doubt that she is the best candidate.
context: Using 'não há dúvida de que' to completely eliminate any room for ambiguity.

front: Estou confuso sobre qual ferramenta usar.
pronunciation: ees-TOY kohn-FOO-zoo SOH-bree kwaw feh-hah-MEHN-tah oo-ZAHR.
back: I am confused about which tool to use.
context: Expressing mental uncertainty. If the speaker is female, the adjective shifts to 'confusa'.

front: É possível que ocorra um atraso na entrega.
pronunciation: eh poo-SEE-vew kee oo-KOH-hah oong ah-TRAH-zoo nah eehn-TREH-gah.
back: It is possible that a delay in delivery occurs.
context: Using 'é possível que' to state low-to-mid certainty. Triggers the subjunctive 'ocorra'.

front: Eu aposto que o plano vai funcionar.
pronunciation: ew ah-POHS-too kee oo PLAH-noo vy foon-syo-NAHR.
back: I bet that the plan will work.
context: Using the colloquial expression 'apostar que' (to bet that) to show confidence in an outcome.

front: Sem dúvida nenhuma, este é o caminho certo.
pronunciation: sayng DOO-vee-dah nee-NYOO-mah, EHS-chee eh oo kah-MEENG-yoo SEHR-too.
back: Without any doubt, this is the right way.
context: Using 'sem dúvida nenhuma' to aggressively reinforce certainty in a choice or path.

front: Fiquei em dúvida entre as duas opções.
pronunciation: fee-KAY ahyng DOO-vee-dah EHN-tree ahs DOO-ahs ohp-SOHNGS.
back: I was in doubt between the two options.
context: Using the past tense phrase 'ficar em dúvida' to express a past state of hesitation or indecision.

front: Não estou totalmente convencido com essa ideia.
pronunciation: nohng ees-TOY toh-taw-MEHN-chee kohn-vehn-SEE-doo koong EHS-ah ee-DEH-yah.
back: I am not totally convinced by this idea.
context: Expressing mild skepticism or lack of complete certainty. Female speakers use 'convencida'.

front: É quase certo que teremos uma nova versão amanhã.
pronunciation: eh KWAH-zee SEHR-too kee teh-REH-moos OO-mah NOH-vah vehr-SOHNG ah-mah-NYAH.
back: It's almost certain that we will have a new version tomorrow.
context: Using 'é quase certo que' to show highly probable certainty backed by structural trends.

front: Isso parece óbvio para todo mundo.
pronunciation: EES-oo pah-REH-see AWB-vyoo PAH-rah TOH-doo MOON-doo.
back: That seems obvious to everyone.
context: Stating that a concept requires no doubt or explanation because it is transparent ('óbvio').

front: Suspeito que há um erro nessa linha.
pronunciation: soos-PAY-too kee ah oong EH-hoo NEHS-ah LEENG-yah.
back: I suspect there is an error in this line.
context: Using 'suspeitar que' to voice a tentative, unverified hunch in code or administrative tasks.

front: É impossível terminar todo o trabalho em uma hora.
pronunciation: eh eeym-poo-SEE-vew tehr-mee-NAHR TOH-doo oo trah-BAH-lyoo ahyng OO-mah OH-rah.
back: It's impossible to finish all the work in one hour.
context: Expressing absolute negative certainty regarding capacity or execution bounds.

front: Tenho um pressentimento bom sobre este projeto.
pronunciation: TEYNG-yoo oong preh-sehn-chee-MEHN-too boong SOH-bree EHS-chee proo-ZHEH-too.
back: I have a good feeling about this project.
context: Using 'pressentimento' (hunch/feeling) to share intuitive, informal optimism.

front: Não resta a menor dúvida sobre o resultado.
pronunciation: nohng HEHS-tah ah meh-NYOHR DOO-vee-dah SOH-bree oo heh-zool-TAH-doo.
back: There isn't the slightest doubt left about the result.
context: A emphatic phrase confirming a clear conclusion using the verb 'restar' (to remain).

front: Pode ser que eu precise de ajuda com o framework.
pronunciation: POH-jee sayr kee ew prah-SEE-zee jee ah-ZHOO-dah koong oo fraym-vohrk.
back: It could be that I need help with the framework.
context: Using 'Pode ser que...' (It could be that / transparently possible) which triggers the subjunctive 'precise'.

front: Quem sabe a gente não viaja no próximo mês?
pronunciation: kayng SAH-bee ah ZHEHN-chee nohng vy-AH-zhah noo PROH-see-moo mays?
back: Who knows, maybe we travel next month?
context: Using the idiomatic alternative phrase 'quem sabe' (who knows / perhaps) to float speculative ideas.

front: Estou cem por cento seguro da minha decisão.
pronunciation: ees-TOY sayng poor SEHN-too seh-GOO-roo dah MEENG-yah jee-see-ZOHNG.
back: I am one hundred percent sure of my decision.
context: A colloquial metric used to establish complete confidence. Female speakers use 'segura'.

front: Isso me deixa um pouco inseguro.
pronunciation: EES-oo mee DAY-shah oong POH-koo eeyng-seh-GOO-roo.
back: That makes me a bit insecure/uncertain.
context: Admitting an internal sense of doubt or professional hesitation. Female speakers use 'insegura'.

front: Caso você mude de ideia, me avise.
pronunciation: KAH-zoo voh-SEH MOO-jee jee ee-DEH-yah, mee ah-VEE-zee.
back: In case you change your mind, let me know.
context: Using the conditional element 'caso' (in case) which mandates the subjunctive form 'mude'.

front: Provavelmente o cliente vai aceitar os termos.
pronunciation: proo-vah-vew-MEHN-chee oo klee-EHN-chee vy ah-say-TAHR ooz TEHR-moos.
back: Probably the client will accept the terms.
context: Using the standalone adverb 'provavelmente' to outline high circumstantial likelihood.

front: Isso é altamente improvável no momento.
pronunciation: EES-oo eh aw-tah-MEHN-chee eeym-proo-VAH-vew noo moo-MEHN-too.
back: That is highly improbable at the moment.
context: Stating deep structural doubt regarding an event's likelihood using 'improvável'.

front: Estou convicto de que tomamos a escolha certa.
pronunciation: ees-TOY kohn-VEEK-too jee kee toh-MAH-moos ah ees-KOH-lyah SEHR-tah.
back: I am convinced that we made the right choice.
context: A formal assertion of certainty using 'convicto' (convinced/persuaded). Female: 'convicta'.

front: De uma coisa eu sei: o prazo não muda.
pronunciation: jee OO-mah KOY-zah ew say: oo PRAH-zoo nohng MOO-dah.
back: One thing I know: the deadline doesn't change.
context: An idiomatic conversational pivot phrase used to establish an absolute foundational fact.

front: Resta saber se temos orçamento para isso.
pronunciation: HEHS-tah sah-BEHR see TEH-moos oohr-sah-MEHN-too PAH-rah EES-oo.
back: It remains to be seen if we have a budget for this.
context: Using the analytical construct 'resta saber se' (it remains to be known/seen if) to state macro uncertainty.

front: Dou minha palavra de que tudo vai dar certo.
pronunciation: dow MEENG-yah pah-LAH-vrah jee kee TOO-doo vy dahr SEHR-too.
back: I give my word that everything is going to work out.
context: Pledging complete relational certainty and personal backing using 'dar minha palavra'.
